message:
feat(observability): add spectral runtime health monitor; fix net/runtime formatting and test correctness

New module — src/observability/spectral_health.rs (1959 lines):
  Graph Laplacian-based runtime health diagnostics that computes the
  Fiedler value (second-smallest eigenvalue of the Laplacian) over the
  task/region dependency graph. Near-zero Fiedler value signals an
  approaching deadlock (graph disconnection); large values indicate
  healthy redundant connectivity. Also detects oscillatory eigenvalue
  trajectories as potential livelock indicators. Includes Cheeger
  inequality-based edge expansion bounds and bottleneck identification
  via the Fiedler vector. Registered in observability/mod.rs.

Formatting and correctness fixes across net/runtime:

- http/h1/listener.rs: Reformat long sleep() call with transient
  accept backoff to respect line-width limits.

- net/tcp/stream.rs: Remove trailing whitespace. Remove unreachable
  `continue` at end of for-loop body in connect(). Reformat
  connect_timeout signature and wait_for_connect_fallback timer
  chain for readability.

- net/unix/stream.rs: Same timer chain reformat as TCP variant.

- runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs: Replace assert_ne!(hash, 0) with
  a determinism check (hash_a == hash_b) — the old test could
  theoretically pass with a non-deterministic hasher, and the intent
  is to verify that witness_hash() is stable across calls.

- runtime/state.rs: Prefix unused `other` match arm binding with
  underscore (_other) and remove tracing_compat::error! call before
  unreachable!() — the error log is never observable since
  unreachable panics immediately.

- tests/net_hardening.rs: Reformat connect_timeout call to respect
  line width.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
